_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of hardware asset management principles and practices
reference_document_element_description: Maintain and optimize existing BI assets, including performance tuning, data model updates, and deprecation of unused content.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI AB domain. Task AB-T-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-016 (DQ, cosine: 0.474), RM-K-011 (RM, cosine: 0.470).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 1.7/10 (raw 0.173). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [asset, hardware, knowledge, management, practices, principles] vs WIDAI-only terms [assets, bi, content, data, deprecation, existing] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.857 (P=0.863, R=0.850). Balanced P/R — the 8-word NICE element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.5%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of hardware asset management principles and practices" and "Maintain and optimize existing BI assets, including performance tuning, data mod". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.475 > STS 0.173 (gap +0.302). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.1%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [asset, hardware, knowledge, management, practices, principles]. WIDAI terms: [assets, bi, content, data, deprecation, existing].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.5% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.302 with low STS 0.173. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
